title: Parent — Report Cards & Self-Study Progress
content:
Overview


The Report Cards page allows parents to generate and view official report cards for each child by term. The Self-Study Progress page tracks independent learning activity including course progress and quiz attempts.



Report Cards Page


Report cards are official documents showing a child's grades for a specific term. Key features:


  • Child sub-tabs: Select which child to generate a report card for
  • Term selector: Choose the academic term (Fall Semester, Spring Semester, Winter Semester, September Term, January Term, May Term)
  • Year selector: Use plus/minus buttons to change the academic year

Generating a Report Card:


  1. Select the child from the sub-tabs
  2. Choose the term and year
  3. The report card displays automatically, showing:
    • School name and information
    • Student name and grade level
    • All subjects with grades for the selected term
    • Teacher comments where available
    • Term and year clearly displayed

The report card view is a formatted display intended for printing or saving. Parents can use their browser's print function to create a physical copy or save as PDF.


If no grades exist for the selected term and year, a message indicates that grades are not yet available.



Self-Study Progress Page


This page tracks the child's independent learning through the platform's self-study courses. It is separate from school-assigned grades. Key features:


  • Child sub-tabs: Select which child's progress to view

The page is divided into three sections:


Course Progress (Left Card):


  • Shows progress bars for each self-study course the child is enrolled in
  • Each bar displays the course name and completion percentage

Overall Performance (Right Card):


  • Summary statistics including average grade, quizzes completed, and study hours

Recent Quiz Attempts (Bottom Card):


  • Chronological list of recent quizzes the child has taken
  • Shows the subject, quiz name, score, and date for each attempt
  • Scrollable list with the most recent attempts at the top

This information helps parents understand how their child is using the platform independently, beyond assigned schoolwork.



Key Talking Points for Agents


  • "Report cards are term-specific — select the child, term, and year to generate one."
  • "Use your browser's print function to save or print the report card."
  • "If no grades appear, the teacher may not have entered them yet for that term."
  • "Self-Study Progress shows independent learning — it's separate from school grades."
  • "The Recent Quiz Attempts list shows exactly which quizzes your child has taken and how they scored."
